findings.evidence: `rg` finds only the new special cases at `PathSpace/Shift.lean:53` and `:65`; the same arbitrary prefix reindexing shape already exists only as a private helper at `Exchangeability/Contractability.lean:133`, showing the reusable theorem is present but not exposed at the natural level.
findings.fix: Add public general lemmas such as `(pathLaw μ X).map (fun x : ℕ → α => fun k => x (φ k)) = pathLaw μ (fun k ω => X (φ k) ω)` and `(pathLaw μ X).map (fun x => fun i : Fin n => x (k i)) = blockLaw μ X k`, then prove the shift-iterate lemmas as special cases with `φ k = k + r`/`k i = i.val + r`.
findings.issue: `map_shift_iterate_pathLaw` and `map_prefixProj_shift_iterate_pathLaw` are under-generalized to the consecutive reindexing `n ↦ n + r`, while the natural API is arbitrary coordinate reindexing.

summary: The new path-law consumer lemmas are shift-iterate special cases of a reusable arbitrary-reindexing API already needed privately elsewhere. Expose the general path-law/block-law reindex lemmas and derive these shift forms from them.
